blurb:
If you need a reliable breakfast or lunch spot in Sandy Springs, J. Christopher's is the one I keep going back to. It's a daytime-only place, open every day until 2pm, so it's perfect for a lazy weekend morning or a weekday lunch that doesn't feel like a sad desk salad. The vibe is comfortable and low-key, the kind of place where you don't feel rushed.

They do classic breakfast fare really well, think omelets built the way you want them, plus burgers and other American staples for lunch. They also run seasonal specials, so there's usually something a little different to try if you're a regular.

Just keep in mind they close at 2pm every single day, so this is not a dinner option. Plan accordingly.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM, Tuesday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM, Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM, Thursday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM, Friday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM, Saturday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM, Sunday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM

blurb:
Los Bravos has been a go-to for a lot of families in this area for a while now, and the food from the kitchen is the reason why. It's a solid, reliable Mexican spot on Roswell Road that's open every day and won't break the bank for food.

The kitchen gets consistent praise, so it's a good call for a casual family dinner or a quick lunch. The portions are solid and the food is what you'd want from a neighborhood Mexican place.

One heads up from longtime regulars: the margaritas have gotten noticeably pricier. The food is still worth it, but go in knowing the bar tab might surprise you.
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
Round1 at Cumberland Mall is one of those places that works really well for older kids and teenagers, especially on a Friday or Saturday night. It's a big arcade and bowling complex that stays open until 2am, which makes it a solid option when you need something to do late.

The best value is the all-you-can-play game pass for the arcade section in the back. You pay one fee and just go. There's a good mix of classic arcade games and live games to keep people busy for a couple of hours.

If you're going with younger kids, aim for earlier in the day. Evenings on weekends get busy and loud, which is fine for teens but can be a lot for little ones.
hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 2:00 AM, Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 2:00 AM, Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 2:00 AM, Thursday: 10:00 AM – 2:00 AM, Friday: 10:00 AM – 2:00 AM, Saturday: 10:00 AM – 2:00 AM, Sunday: 10:00 AM – 2:00 AM

blurb:
SATE on Roswell Road is a small spot with a big personality. The owner is the kind of guy who actually talks to you about the food, explains where things come from, and makes you feel like a guest rather than just a customer. It's the kind of place that makes you want to come back just for the experience, not only the food.

The pho here is made the authentic way, and you can tell. If you've never tried pho before, this is genuinely a great place to start. It's also an easy, affordable weekday lunch or dinner when you want something warm and filling without spending a lot.

They're open every day from 10am to 9pm, which makes it one of the more flexible options in the neighborhood.
address: 5980 Roswell Rd NE, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
Canton Cooks is right next door to SATE on Roswell Road and it's been a neighborhood standby for years. The menu goes well beyond the standard takeout options, with things like hot pot, BBQ duck, and lobster in black bean sauce that you don't usually see at a casual Chinese spot. Good for a weeknight dinner when you want something more interesting than the usual.

The BBQ duck and the hot pot are the things that set this place apart from other Chinese restaurants nearby. If you're going with a group, hot pot is a fun way to do it.

They're open late every day, until 10pm, which makes them a solid option when you're deciding what to do for dinner at the last minute.
address: 5984 Roswell Rd NE, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Saturday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM, Sunday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM

blurb:
If you're in the mood for pizza and don't want to spend much, JJ's on Roswell Road is the move. It's casual and no-frills, but the pizza is the real deal. Good for a weeknight dinner or grabbing a pie to take home.

The stuffed pizza is what people are talking about, and for good reason. One slice is genuinely filling enough to be a whole meal, and the value is hard to beat. If you've only had their regular pies, it's worth branching out.

Just note they're closed on Tuesdays, and they do deliver if you'd rather stay home.
address: 2211 Roswell Rd suite 116, Marietta, GA 30062, USA

blurb:
This little spot on Roswell Road is something a little different for East Cobb. It's a Brazilian açaí shop, and if you've never had açaí done right, this is a good place to start. The vibe is cozy and the staff is genuinely friendly. Good for an afternoon treat or a post-workout stop.

The açaí is self-serve style, so you build your own bowl with whatever toppings you want. They also have crepes and coxinha, which is a Brazilian fried chicken snack that's worth trying if you've never had one.

They're closed on Tuesdays, so plan accordingly.
address: 2145 Roswell Rd Suite 110, Marietta, GA 30062, USA

blurb:
Sunnyside Pizzeria is genuinely one of the best pizza spots in the area, and I don't say that lightly. It's tucked into a shopping center off Sandy Springs Circle, but once you're inside and the food hits the table, you forget all about the strip mall. Good for a Friday night out, a casual date, or picking up a pie on the way home.

The Hot Honey Char pizza is what gets people hooked, and the Hot Honey Lemon Pepper Chicken Wings are worth ordering every single time. The sauce is that perfect sweet-and-spicy combo that makes you want to dip your crust into whatever's left on the plate. The Caesar Salad is a solid starter too, nothing fancy, just done right.

They're closed on Mondays, so plan around that.
address: 220 Sandy Springs Cir Suite 149, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
Ray's on the River is the place you take someone when you want to make an impression. It sits right on the Chattahoochee over in Sandy Springs, and the view alone makes it feel like a special occasion. This is date night territory, or the kind of place you take out-of-town guests when you want to show off a little.

The menu focuses on Southern seafood, so expect dishes that feel a step above your usual fish dinner. It's not cheap, but for a celebration or anniversary meal, it delivers.

Sunday hours are split with a break between 4pm and 5pm, so check the schedule before you head over.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
Royal Spice on Hammond Drive is my go-to when I want a solid Indian dinner without driving far. The staff is genuinely friendly and patient if you have questions about the menu, which matters when you're trying to navigate a menu full of things you haven't ordered before. It's a good spot for a sit-down dinner, and the room is quiet enough to actually have a conversation.

The entrees are the main draw here, and the appetizers are worth adding to your order rather than skipping. The food is flavorful and they seem to put real care into what comes out of the kitchen.

Note that they close between lunch and dinner on weekdays, roughly 2:30pm to 4:30pm, so it's not a good drop-in option in the middle of the afternoon.
address: 220 Hammond Dr #302, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
